payload.description:
## Behavioral contract

Rewrite `app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx` so that:

1. The page renders as a **gallery grid**, not a list of text-only cards. Each tile is a thumbnail image + title (+ year when present), sized so a user scanning the page can see the work, not just its name.
2. The **existing cursor-follow hover preview is preserved**. Hovering a tile shows the larger floating preview image (same UX as today).
3. Clicking a tile opens a **detail modal** showing every metadata field present for that work — title, year, medium, dimensions, collection, currentLocation, sourceUrl, note — and nothing for fields that are absent (no "N/A", no placeholder rows).
4. If the work's data includes an `articleSlug`, the modal renders a **"Read full article →"** link that navigates to `/<articleSlug>` (site-internal; resolved via the existing catch-all MDX route). If `articleSlug` is absent, no link is shown.

### Required behaviors (testable)

- **Thumbnails load**: every tile shows `/images/duchamp/paintings/<filename>`. Images use `next/image` with `unoptimized` set to match existing behavior (images are already served statically).
- **Grid layout**: responsive grid — multiple columns on wide viewports, collapsing on mobile. Mirror the Tailwind grid class approach already used elsewhere on the site (e.g. `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`). Exact breakpoint counts are implementer's choice, but the **minimum acceptance**: on a 1920px viewport at least 3 tiles per row; on a 375px viewport exactly 1 tile per row.
- **Hover preview preserved**: moving the mouse over a tile shows the same floating image preview already implemented in today's file (cursor-follow, `z-[9999]`, `pointer-events-none`). The preview must not interfere with click-to-open.
- **Click opens modal**: clicking a tile opens a modal overlay (not a new page). The modal has a visible close affordance (X button top-right) and closes on: (a) clicking the X, (b) clicking the overlay backdrop, (c) pressing Escape.
- **Modal contents**: only the fields present in the data for that work are rendered. Labels are human-readable ("Medium:", "Dimensions:", "Collection:", "Location:", "Source:"). The `note` field, when present, renders as a paragraph under the metadata rows. The `sourceUrl` field, when present, renders as an external link with `rel="noopener noreferrer"` and a Lucide `ExternalLink` icon.
- **Article link**: when `articleSlug` is present, the modal's last element is a link styled as a prominent call-to-action ("Read full article →"), navigating to `/<articleSlug>`. The link uses Next.js `<Link>` so it client-side-navigates.

### Implementation notes

- Mirror the modal pattern used by `components/title-cards/card-detail-modal.tsx`. Either reuse that component if it is general enough, or copy its accessibility scaffolding (focus trap, Escape handling, backdrop click) into a new component at `components/duchamp-works/work-detail-modal.tsx`. Do NOT hand-roll modal a11y from scratch.
- Read the data via the loader introduced in `pb-6c8`. Do not import the old `duchampArtworks` const — it will no longer exist.
- The page may remain a client component (`'use client'`), since the hover interaction and modal open/close need client state.

### Acceptance criteria

- Visit `/duchamp-works` in dev: the page renders 67 tiles across the 8 period groups, each with a thumbnail image and title. No tile is text-only.
- Hovering any tile shows the cursor-follow floating preview (same as current behavior).
- Clicking any tile opens a modal; the modal shows the work's title, year (if present), and the filename's image at display size. Metadata rows for medium/dimensions/collection/currentLocation/sourceUrl/note appear **only when those fields are present** in the JSON.
- Clicking outside the modal, clicking its close button, or pressing Escape closes it.
- For a work with `articleSlug` set in the data, the modal shows "Read full article →" linking to `/<articleSlug>`. For a work without, no such link is present.
- `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` pass.
- Keyboard accessibility: tabbing onto a tile + Enter opens the modal; Escape closes it; focus is trapped within the open modal.

### Scope boundaries / non-goals

- **Do NOT** edit or enrich the data file. Assume the JSON only has the legacy three fields populated for existing works (title, year, filename) — the page must render correctly anyway and expose the empty states by simply not showing those metadata rows.
- **Do NOT** add filtering, sorting, or search UI to `/duchamp-works`. That is out of scope for this rework.
- **Do NOT** introduce pagination. 67 rows render fine on a single page.
- **Do NOT** touch other gallery-style pages (`/gatherer`, etc.) in this pebble.
- **Do NOT** add an API route. The data is already in the client import path once `pb-6c8` lands.

### Regression gate

The pre-rework behavior was: text-only cards with a hover preview but no modal. The post-rework page must NOT silently reduce to that — at least one acceptance criterion above (the click-opens-modal behavior) verifies the new capability exists.

### Why

Today's `/duchamp-works` page shows 67 untitled-by-default text cards that require a hover to show even the image. A user browsing the gallery cannot scan for visual recognition, cannot read scholarship notes in place, and cannot jump to any longer-form treatment of a work. This rework turns the page into a scannable gallery with click-to-detail access — the standard museum-catalog pattern — while preserving the hover-preview as a secondary affordance. The page becomes a template that the site can reuse for future catalog pages without reinventing the modal + hover + grid plumbing.

### Preconditions

- `pb-6c8` (data migration + schema) must be complete. This pebble imports from that loader and uses the enriched field list.
payload.title: Rewrite /duchamp-works as gallery grid with click-to-open detail modal

payload.description:
## Behavioral contract

Write a short, author-facing README at `data/README.md` that documents the `duchamp-artworks.json` schema and editing workflow. The audience is a non-engineer collaborator who wants to add a new Duchamp work, update an existing one, or link a work to a full article, **without touching TypeScript or React**.

### Required sections

1. **Where data lives**: state that Duchamp catalog data is `data/duchamp-artworks.json`, the schema is enforced by `lib/duchamp-artworks-schema.ts`, and validation runs at build time.

2. **Schema reference**: list every field (`title`, `year`, `filename`, `medium`, `dimensions`, `collection`, `currentLocation`, `sourceUrl`, `note`, `articleSlug`) with:
   - required vs. optional
   - expected value shape (one-line example)
   - any constraint the schema enforces (e.g. note max 200 words, sourceUrl must be a URL, articleSlug has no leading slash)

3. **"How to add a work"**: a numbered 5–10 step recipe showing exactly where to paste a new entry inside the JSON, what the minimum required fields are (title, year, filename), and the filename convention (kebab-case with year suffix, lives under `public/images/duchamp/paintings/`).

4. **"How to link a work to a full article"**: show a one-line example using `articleSlug`, explain that the slug resolves against the site's catch-all MDX route (so any MDX under `content/pages/<slug>.mdx` is reachable), and point the author at the MDX authoring pattern (a one-line reference to `content/pages/impossible-bed-i.mdx` is sufficient; do NOT duplicate MDX documentation here).

5. **"What happens if I break the schema"**: one paragraph explaining that a validation failure will surface as a build error with the field path and reason, and what a typical error message looks like.

### Acceptance criteria

- `data/README.md` exists and renders readably on GitHub (standard markdown, no exotic syntax).
- Every field in the Zod schema from `pb-6c8` is listed in the schema reference section.
- The "How to add a work" recipe is testable: an engineer could follow it and successfully add a new entry without reading any source code.
- The word-count cap on `note` is stated explicitly with the number.
- The README does not duplicate MDX-authoring guidance that lives in the existing CLAUDE.md or anywhere else — it links to those instead.

### Scope boundaries / non-goals

- **Do NOT** write a schema reference for any other data file (`title-cards.json`, etc.). This README is scoped to `duchamp-artworks.json` only.
- **Do NOT** introduce a build step to auto-generate docs from the Zod schema. Hand-authored is fine; the schema is small.
- **Do NOT** add a README anywhere else in the repo.

### Why

The rationale for rewriting `/duchamp-works` as a data-driven page is lost if the author still needs engineer-assistance to add a row. This pebble exists to make the new architecture **usable by non-engineers** — without the README, adding a work still feels like a code change even though the JSON lives outside of `/lib`. This is the same reason the pb-695 MDX migration relies on a clear content/ directory structure: the author edits content, the page renders it, and documentation is the last mile that makes that contract real.

### Preconditions

- `pb-6c8` (schema + JSON) must be complete, so the README can reference the final field set.
- This pebble does not block `pb-ac9` (the page rewrite); both can proceed in parallel once `pb-6c8` is done.
payload.title: Author-facing README for data/duchamp-artworks.json schema and editing workflow

payload.description:
## Behavioral contract

Replace the **hardcoded data literal** inside `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` with a JSON data file validated at read time by a Zod schema. Enrich the per-work schema with real catalog metadata fields so future work can render rich detail when data is present and degrade gracefully when it is not.

The public API of `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` must be preserved: the existing named export `duchampArtworks` must remain callable by downstream consumers (`app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx`, `components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx`) without them needing to change. After this task, `duchampArtworks` resolves to the validated JSON at module load rather than to a hardcoded array — but the identifier, the shape it points to, and the import path all stay the same.

### Required deliverables

1. **New data file**: `data/duchamp-artworks.json`. Must contain every period and every work currently in `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts`, with the existing fields (`title`, `year`, `filename`, period `title`, period `years`) preserved verbatim for all 67 works. None of the new optional metadata fields need to be populated for any existing work — schema capacity only.

2. **New schema file**: `lib/duchamp-artworks-schema.ts`. Must export a Zod schema and the derived TypeScript types. The per-work schema adds the following **optional** fields on top of the existing three:
   - `medium` — string, optional. Example: "Oil on canvas".
   - `dimensions` — string, optional. Example: "54 × 55 cm".
   - `collection` — string, optional. Example: "Philadelphia Museum of Art".
   - `currentLocation` — string, optional. Example: "Gallery 182".
   - `sourceUrl` — string (URL), optional. External canonical page for the work.
   - `note` — string, optional. Short descriptive blurb. **Max 200 words.** Enforce with `.refine` in the schema.
   - `articleSlug` — string, optional. Site-internal slug (e.g. `"impossible-bed-i"`) that resolves via the existing catch-all MDX route. Must not include a leading slash; enforce this in the schema. Existence of the target MDX is NOT validated at build time.

3. **New type export**: `types/duchamp-artworks.ts` that re-exports the derived Zod types (`DuchampArtwork`, `DuchampPeriod`). Mirrors the `types/title-cards.ts` convention used by `/gatherer`.

4. **Reworked loader module**: `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` replaces its 240-line hardcoded literal with a JSON-backed, validated-at-load computed value. Specifically:
   - Import `data/duchamp-artworks.json`.
   - Parse + validate it against the Zod schema at module load.
   - **Throw at import time on any schema violation** so a malformed edit fails the build, not runtime.
   - **Preserve the existing named export `duchampArtworks`** so current importers (page.tsx, artwork-card.tsx, anything else using this identifier) continue working without modification. It now resolves to the validated result.
   - Optionally also export a function form (`getDuchampArtworks()`) if that simplifies the implementation, but the named const form is the load-bearing API.

5. **Delete the hardcoded data literal** from `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts`. The 240-line const array goes away. What remains is: imports, the validated computed value, the named export.

### Acceptance criteria

- `data/duchamp-artworks.json` exists; all 67 works are present; every legacy field that was non-empty before is non-empty now.
- `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` no longer contains the hardcoded `duchampArtworks: DuchampPeriod[] = [...]` literal.
- `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` still exports `duchampArtworks` as a named export; no call-site anywhere in the repo needs to change to still type-check.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes.
- `npm run lint` passes.
- `/duchamp-works` renders the full list in dev (verify by spinning dev server and fetching the page).
- The schema rejects: a `note` over 200 words, an `articleSlug` with a leading slash, a malformed `sourceUrl`. Demonstrate with a throwaway script (can live in `/tmp`; no new test framework needs to be introduced).
- Importing `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` with a deliberately broken `data/duchamp-artworks.json` throws a schema error at module load time (can be demonstrated with a temporary corruption + revert).

### Scope boundaries / non-goals

- **Do NOT** edit `app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx` or `components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx`. They continue to import `{ duchampArtworks }` from `lib/duchamp-artworks.ts` and must keep working without modification. Call-site rewrites belong to `pb-695.15.2`.
- **Do NOT** populate any of the new optional metadata fields on existing works. The schema adds capacity; backfill is an author task that happens later.
- **Do NOT** add an API route. The gallery page can import the module directly.
- **Do NOT** move images or change image paths.
- **Do NOT** reformat unrelated files. If a formatter runs on save/startup in the worktree, revert those unrelated diffs before committing. Only files directly produced by this task should appear in the final commit.

### Regression gate

The pre-task state had `duchampArtworks` as a hardcoded literal typed by TS. The post-task state must have `duchampArtworks` as a JSON-backed, Zod-validated value typed via the schema's inferred type. A violation of the schema in the JSON file must fail the build; it must NOT silently fall back to any hardcoded default. At least one acceptance criterion (the import-time throw) verifies this explicitly.

### Why

This pebble reduces the marginal cost of maintaining catalog data on the site. Storing structured data in a `.ts` module couples the data layer to the type system and discourages non-engineer authors from editing. Moving to JSON + Zod gives us: (a) author-friendly data editing, (b) runtime validation at the boundary, (c) parity with the existing `/gatherer` pattern (`data/title-cards.json` + `types/title-cards.ts`), and (d) an extensible schema that can grow without touching consumer code. This is the foundation task — `pb-695.15.2` (the page rewrite) depends on the enriched schema existing, and `pb-695.15.3` (the author README) depends on the final field set being stable.

Preserving the `duchampArtworks` named export is deliberate: it keeps the scope of this pebble narrow to "data layer only" and lets `pb-695.15.2` evolve the call sites alongside the new gallery UI, rather than forcing a partial, awkward call-site migration here.

## Behavioral contract

Build a **new implementation of the Duchamp works gallery at a preview path**, leaving the existing production page untouched so the coordinator (Eric) can review the two side by side before any cutover. This task does NOT replace `/duchamp-works`. It stands up a second, parallel page at a new URL.

Specifically:

- The new page lives at `app/(site)/duchamp-works-preview/page.tsx` and renders at `/duchamp-works-preview` in dev and preview deployments.
- `app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx` is **not** modified, renamed, or deleted in this pebble.
- `components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx` is **not** modified in this pebble (it is still consumed by the current `/duchamp-works`).
- The cutover (delete old page, promote preview to the canonical route) is a separate pebble (`pb-695.15.4`) that only runs after human review.

The preview page itself must:

1. Render as a **gallery grid**, not a list of text-only cards. Each tile is a thumbnail image + title (+ year when present), sized so a user scanning the page can see the work, not just its name.
2. Preserve the **cursor-follow hover preview** from the current page — hovering a tile shows the larger floating preview image (same UX as today). This is a second affordance, not a replacement for click.
3. Open a **detail modal** when a tile is clicked. The modal shows every metadata field that is present for that work and nothing for fields that are absent (no "N/A", no empty rows). Fields to surface: title, year, medium, dimensions, collection, currentLocation, sourceUrl, note.
4. When a work's data has `articleSlug`, the modal renders a **"Read full article →"** link that navigates to `/<articleSlug>` via Next.js `<Link>` (resolved by the existing catch-all MDX route). When `articleSlug` is absent, no such link appears.

### Required behaviors (testable)

- **Thumbnails load**: every tile shows `/images/duchamp/paintings/<filename>`. Images use `next/image` with `unoptimized` set to match existing behavior (images are already served statically).
- **Grid layout**: responsive grid — multiple columns on wide viewports, collapsing on mobile. Mirror the Tailwind grid class approach already used elsewhere on the site (e.g. `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4`). Exact breakpoint counts are implementer's choice. Minimum acceptance: on a 1920px viewport at least 3 tiles per row; on a 375px viewport exactly 1 tile per row.
- **Hover preview preserved**: moving the mouse over a tile shows the floating cursor-follow image preview (`z-[9999]`, `pointer-events-none`). The preview must not interfere with click-to-open.
- **Click opens modal**: clicking a tile opens a modal overlay (not a new page). The modal has a visible close affordance (X button top-right) and closes on: (a) clicking the X, (b) clicking the overlay backdrop, (c) pressing Escape.
- **Modal contents**: only the fields present in the data for that work are rendered. Labels are human-readable ("Medium:", "Dimensions:", "Collection:", "Location:", "Source:"). The `note` field, when present, renders as a paragraph under the metadata rows. The `sourceUrl` field, when present, renders as an external link with `rel="noopener noreferrer"` and a Lucide `ExternalLink` icon.
- **Article link**: when `articleSlug` is present, the modal's last element is a link styled as a prominent call-to-action ("Read full article →"), navigating to `/<articleSlug>`. Client-side navigation via Next.js `<Link>`.

### Implementation notes

- Mirror the modal pattern used by `components/title-cards/card-detail-modal.tsx`. Either reuse that component if it is general enough, or copy its accessibility scaffolding (focus trap, Escape handling, backdrop click) into a new component at `components/duchamp-works/work-detail-modal.tsx`. Do NOT hand-roll modal a11y from scratch.
- Read the data via the loader from `pb-695.15.1`: `import { duchampArtworks } from '@/lib/duchamp-artworks'` (the named export is preserved by that pebble). Types come from `@/types/duchamp-artworks` or `@/lib/duchamp-artworks-schema`.
- The preview page may remain a client component (`'use client'`) since the hover interaction and modal open/close need client state.
- If you need shared UI subcomponents (tile, modal), place them under `components/duchamp-works/`. Do NOT place them under `components/artworks/` — that directory still belongs to the old page until cutover.

### Acceptance criteria

- Visit `/duchamp-works-preview` in dev: the page renders 67 tiles across the 4 period groups (Early Works, Major Period, Readymades & Later Works, Undated Works), each with a thumbnail image and title. No tile is text-only.
- Visit `/duchamp-works` in dev: the page is **unchanged from its current behavior** — same text-only cards, same cursor-follow hover preview, same absence of a modal. This acts as the regression gate against accidental edits to the production path.
- On the preview page: hovering any tile shows the cursor-follow floating preview.
- On the preview page: clicking any tile opens a modal; the modal shows the work's title, year (if present), and the filename's image at display size. Metadata rows for medium/dimensions/collection/currentLocation/sourceUrl/note appear **only when those fields are present** in the JSON. Since backfill is an author task deferred by `pb-695.15.1`, expect most modals today to show only title/year/image.
- Clicking outside the modal, clicking its close button, or pressing Escape closes it.
- For a work with `articleSlug` set (temporarily add one to one JSON entry to verify, then revert), the modal shows "Read full article →" linking to `/<articleSlug>`. For a work without, no such link is present.
- `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` pass.
- Keyboard accessibility: tabbing onto a tile + Enter opens the modal; Escape closes it; focus is trapped within the open modal.

### Scope boundaries / non-goals

- **Do NOT** edit `app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx`. It must keep rendering the current production UI unchanged. Cutover is in `pb-695.15.4`.
- **Do NOT** edit `components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx`. It belongs to the current production page.
- **Do NOT** enrich `data/duchamp-artworks.json`. Populating metadata is an author task. The page renders whatever fields happen to be present.
- **Do NOT** add filtering, sorting, search, or pagination UI. Out of scope.
- **Do NOT** add an API route. Import the loader directly.
- **Do NOT** introduce analytics, feature flags, or telemetry for the preview path.
- **Do NOT** reformat unrelated files. If a formatter runs on save/startup in the worktree, revert those unrelated diffs before committing. Only files directly produced by this task should appear in the final commit.

### Regression gate

The acceptance criterion "/duchamp-works is unchanged from its current behavior" is the explicit regression gate. A worker that accidentally edits the production page fails the task regardless of whether the preview path also works.

### Why

Today's `/duchamp-works` page shows 67 untitled-by-default text cards that require a hover to show even the image. A user browsing the gallery cannot scan for visual recognition, cannot read scholarship notes in place, and cannot jump to any longer-form treatment of a work. This rework turns the page into a scannable gallery with click-to-detail access — the standard museum-catalog pattern — while preserving the hover-preview as a secondary affordance. The page becomes a template the site can reuse for future catalog pages without reinventing the modal + hover + grid plumbing.

Building at a preview path (rather than in-place rewriting) mirrors the Phase 1 MDX migration protocol: worker produces preview, coordinator compares side-by-side, cutover is a separate deliberate step. This protects against aesthetic regressions the worker can't judge on its own.

### Preconditions

- `pb-695.15.1` (data migration + schema) must be complete. This pebble imports from that loader and uses the enriched field list.

## Behavioral contract

Cut the Duchamp works gallery over from its preview path to the canonical route. After this pebble, `/duchamp-works` serves the new gallery UI from `pb-695.15.2`, and the old text-card implementation is deleted.

This pebble runs ONLY after the coordinator (Eric) has reviewed `/duchamp-works-preview` side-by-side with `/duchamp-works` and explicitly approved the cutover. Do not spawn a worker for this task before that approval exists.

### Required deliverables

1. **Delete the old production page**: `git rm app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx`.

2. **Delete the now-unused artwork card component** (if and only if nothing else imports it): `git rm components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx`. Verify with a repo-wide grep that no other file imports from `@/components/artworks/artwork-card` before removing. If anything else still depends on it, leave it and flag to the coordinator instead of deleting.

3. **Relocate the preview implementation to the canonical route**:
   - Rename the preview directory: `app/(site)/duchamp-works-preview/` → `app/(site)/duchamp-works/`. Use `git mv` so history is preserved.
   - Update any **absolute import paths** inside the moved files that still reference `duchamp-works-preview` (e.g. `@/components/duchamp-works-preview/...` if that exists) so they point to their new locations.
   - If `pb-695.15.2` placed shared subcomponents under `components/duchamp-works/` (as instructed), no component move is necessary. Only the page directory rename is required.

4. **Nothing else moves**. The data file, schema, types, and loader from `pb-695.15.1` are already at canonical paths and do not change.

### Acceptance criteria

- `/duchamp-works` renders the gallery UI (thumbnails, hover preview, click-to-open modal) — identical behavior to what `/duchamp-works-preview` served before this cutover.
- `/duchamp-works-preview` returns a 404 (Next.js behavior for a removed route). Do NOT leave the preview directory behind with a redirect shim; clean removal.
- `app/(site)/duchamp-works/page.tsx` exists (the relocated new implementation).
- `components/artworks/artwork-card.tsx` either: (a) is deleted and no repo-wide grep finds any remaining importer, or (b) was left in place because a non-gallery file still imports it — document which case applies in the commit message.
- `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass.
- Dev server fetch of `/duchamp-works` returns HTTP 200 with gallery markup (at least one known work title present in the HTML).

### Scope boundaries / non-goals

- **Do NOT** edit the gallery UI itself. Content and behavior are fixed by `pb-695.15.2` and approved at review. This pebble is pure relocation + deletion.
- **Do NOT** add redirects from old paths. `/duchamp-works-preview` never shipped publicly; a plain 404 is correct.
- **Do NOT** touch any data file or schema. Changes there are out of scope.
- **Do NOT** reformat unrelated files.

### Regression gate

The new `/duchamp-works` must visually and behaviorally match what the coordinator approved at `/duchamp-works-preview`. Any silent divergence introduced by the move (e.g. a dropped import, a renamed component that lost its client boundary) is a regression this pebble must catch before closing. At minimum: spin the dev server and verify thumbnails render, hover shows the preview, clicking opens the modal, and an `articleSlug` link (if any exists in the data) still navigates correctly.

### Why

Preview-then-cutover is the Phase 1 discipline: review happens against a parallel path so the coordinator can A/B the new and old without guessing. Once approved, the cutover is a mechanical rename + delete — a single-purpose pebble keeps that mechanical step separate from the design decisions that happened in `pb-695.15.2`, so a bad cutover doesn't corrupt the review record.

### Preconditions

- `pb-695.15.2` is complete AND the coordinator has explicitly approved `/duchamp-works-preview` against `/duchamp-works` side-by-side.
payload.title: Cut over /duchamp-works to the approved gallery preview
